reasoning: The question asks for the probability that the daily average temperature on a future date (presumably November 25, 2025, based on the close date) will be higher than the temperature on October 26, 2025 at Poitiers-Biard Airport, France. Based on historical climate normals for Poitiers, the late October daily mean temperature is approximately 11.0°C, and the late November daily mean is approximately 7.0°C. Daily mean temperatures in this region during autumn typically have a standard deviation around 3.5 to 4.5°C. Assuming the daily temperatures are independent and approximately normally distributed as T1 ~ N(11.0, 4.0^2) and T2 ~ N(7.0, 4.0^2), the difference T2 - T1 follows N(-4.0, 32). The standard deviation of the difference is roughly 5.65°C. Calculating the probability that T2 - T1 > 0 yields a Z-score of +4.0 / 5.65 ≈ +0.71, which corresponds to a right-tail probability of about 24%. Refining the standard deviation estimation slightly downward pushes this to the 20-22% range, taking into account ocean buffering. Since the prompt implies a single date answer and overrides tooling prompts, I forecast roughly 0.22.
